title: Income Approach: If a property has a capitalization rate (cap rate) of...
summary: Calculate property value by dividing NOI by the capitalization rate.
prompt: If a property has a capitalization rate (cap rate) of 8% and a net operating income (NOI) of $500,000, what is the estimated value of the property?
solution: The value of a property using the capitalization rate method can be calculated using the formula: Value = NOI / Cap Rate. Here, Value = $500,000 / 0.08 = $6,250,000. This method is essential for real estate investors to quickly assess property values based on expected income streams. Understanding cap rates and their application in valuation is a fundamental concept for finance students in real estate investment analysis and decision-making.
